title: Linear Thermal Expansion of Structural Metals in Civil Applications
content: Thermal expansion coefficient α: structural steel 12×10⁻⁶/°C, concrete 10×10⁻⁶/°C, aluminium 23×10⁻⁶/°C. Free expansion: ΔL=α·L₀·ΔT. In restrained frames, thermal stress σ=E·α·ΔT. Bridge expansion joints are sized for ΔT=50–80 °C seasonal range (temperate climate). A 20 m steel girder expands 12 mm over a 50 °C temperature swing; joint gaps that do not track this model indicate bearing lock or hidden restraint.
